descriptions: Overlords attacked Indian Posse, which retaliated with a drive-by shooting.
edges.evidence_quote: On the night of 9 February 1994, a rival gang called the Overlords fired a shotgun at an Indian Posse house, leading to a drive-by shooting in retaliation a few hours later that left a woman wounded.

descriptions: Manitoba Warriors absorbed the Overlords gang.
edges.evidence_quote: the need for allies led to the Warriors to absorb the Overlords gang into their ranks

Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb.description: Islamist militant organization active in North Africa and the Sahel, originating from the Salafist Group for Preaching and Combat (GSPC) which rebranded in 2007. It is an affiliate of al-Qaeda, primarily composed of Algerian and Saharan members, and funds itself largely through kidnapping for ransom. The group has been designated a terrorist organization by numerous countries and international bodies. In 2017, its Sahara branch merged with other groups to form Jama'at Nasr al-Islam wal Muslimin.

descriptions: AQIM is a direct affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Qaeda has the following direct affiliates: - Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb (AQIM)

descriptions: AQIM is a group (affiliate) of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Group: Groups: JNIM, AQAP, AQIM, Al-Shabaab, AQIS, Ansaru

descriptions: AQIM is listed as a group under Al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Groups: JNIM , AQAP , AQIM , Al-Shabaab , AQIS , Ansaru

descriptions: Al-Qaeda approved the group's name and franchised it.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamist rebels in the Sahara calling themselves al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb stepped up their violence in the years before 2009. French officials say the rebels have no real links to the al-Qaeda leadership, but this has been disputed. It seems likely that bin Laden approved the group's name in late 2006, and the rebels "took on the al-Qaeda franchise label"

descriptions: Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb is an ally of AQAP.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies ... al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb (2009–present)

descriptions: AQAP and AQIM are allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies ... al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb (2009–present)

descriptions: AQAP lists AQIM as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies al-Shabaab (2009–present), al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb (2009–present)

descriptions: Al-Mourabitoun rejoined AQIM in 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: In December 2015, Belmokhtar's splinter group, Al-Mourabitoun rejoined AQIM, according to audio statements released by both groups.

descriptions: Al-Mulathameen is an ally of AQIM.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies Al-Mulathameen (2012–2013)

descriptions: AQIM is a part of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: is an Islamist militant organization (of al-Qaeda)

descriptions: Al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula is an ally of AQIM.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula (2009–2017)

descriptions: AQIM is suspected of having links with al-Shabaab.
edges.evidence_quote: The group has also been suspected of having links with the Horn of Africa -based militant group al-Shabaab.

descriptions: Ansar al-Sharia (Libya) is an ally of AQIM.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies Ansar al-Sharia (Libya) (2012–2017)

descriptions: Ansar al-Sharia (Tunisia) is an ally of AQIM.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies Ansar al-Sharia (Tunisia) (2011–2017)

descriptions: AQIM's Sahara branch merged with Ansar Dine into JNIM.
edges.evidence_quote: On 2 March 2017, the Sahara branch of AQIM merged with Macina Liberation Front, Ansar Dine, and al-Mourabitoun, into Jama'at Nasr al-Islam wal Muslimin.

descriptions: Ansaru is an ally of AQIM.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies Ansaru (2012–2017)

descriptions: Al-Qaeda in the Sinai Peninsula is an ally of AQIM.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies Al-Qaeda in the Sinai Peninsula (2007–2017)

descriptions: AQIM, al-Shabaab, and Boko Haram attempted to coordinate activities.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb, the Somalia-based Al-Shabaab, and the Nigeria-based Boko Haram were as of June 2012 attempting to synchronize and coordinate their activities in terms of sharing funds, training and explosives.

descriptions: Islamic State Libya Province is an opponent of AQIM.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Non-state opponents Islamic State Libya Province

descriptions: AQIM's Sahara branch merged into JNIM.
edges.evidence_quote: On 2 March 2017, the Sahara branch of AQIM merged with Macina Liberation Front, Ansar Dine, and al-Mourabitoun, into Jama'at Nasr al-Islam wal Muslimin.

descriptions: AQIM's Sahara branch merged with Macina Liberation Front into JNIM.
edges.evidence_quote: On 2 March 2017, the Sahara branch of AQIM merged with Macina Liberation Front, Ansar Dine, and al-Mourabitoun, into Jama'at Nasr al-Islam wal Muslimin.

edges.target: Movement for Oneness and Jihad in West Africa
descriptions: Movement for Oneness and Jihad in West Africa is a splinter group from AQIM.
edges.evidence_quote: In late 2011, the splinter group Movement for Oneness and Jihad in West Africa was founded in order to spread jihadi activities further into West Africa. Their military leader is Omar Ould Hamaha, a former AQIM fighter.

descriptions: Sahara Emirate Brigade is a group within AQIM until 2017.
edges.evidence_quote: Groups: Uqba ibn Nafi Brigade, Sahara Emirate Brigade (until 2017), Al-Mourabitoun Battalion (2015-2017)

descriptions: Uqba ibn Nafi Brigade is a group within AQIM.
edges.evidence_quote: Groups: Uqba ibn Nafi Brigade, Sahara Emirate Brigade (until 2017), Al-Mourabitoun Battalion (2015-2017)

descriptions: Al-Shabaab has forged ties with AQIM.
edges.evidence_quote: has forged ties with other insurgent groups like AQIM and AQAP

descriptions: Al-Shabaab forged ties with AQIM.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Shabab members pledged allegiance to al-Qaeda in 2012 and has reportedly forged ties with al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb , and al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula .

descriptions: Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb is listed as a non-state ally of Boko Haram from 2007 to 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies Taliban, al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb (2007–2015)

descriptions: Observers speculated Boko Haram was affiliated with AQIM.
edges.evidence_quote: The increased sophistication of the group led observers to speculate that Boko Haram was affiliated with Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb (AQIM), which was active in Niger.

descriptions: Boko Haram listed AQIM as an ally from 2007 to 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies Taliban , al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb (2007–2015)

descriptions: Boko Haram and AQIM were allies from 2007 to 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies ... al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb (2007–2015)

descriptions: AQIM is listed as a non-state ally of Boko Haram from 2007 to 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies Taliban, al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb (2007–2015)

descriptions: Boko Haram has maintained a relationship with AQIM for training and material support.
edges.evidence_quote: According to the UN Security Council listing of Boko Haram under the al-Qaeda sanctions regime in May 2014, the group 'has maintained a relationship with AQIM for training and material support purposes'.

descriptions: AQIM was an ally of Boko Haram from 2007 to 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies ... al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb (2007–2015)

Wolfpack Alliance.description: Canadian organized crime group founded in 2010 in British Columbia by Hells Angels member Larry Amero and others. It is a loosely allied consortium of mostly millennial-aged gangsters involved in drug trafficking, money laundering, and murder. The group distributes cocaine from the Sinaloa Cartel and operates across Canada, including British Columbia, Ontario, and Quebec.

descriptions: Jamie Bacon condemned the Wolfpack Alliance, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Bacon condemned the Wolfpack Alliance together with the Indo-Canadian Brothers' Keepers gang in racist and homophobic language.

descriptions: Wolfpack alliance aligned with Brothers Keepers.
edges.evidence_quote: He and his crew of Troy McKinnon and Cody Sleigh were Independent Soldiers members, who, along with the rest of the Independent Soldiers gang and the Wolfpack alliance, would become rivals with the Red Scorpions and BIBO gang while aligning themselves with the Brothers Keepers gang.

descriptions: Dhak-Duhre coalition waged war against the Wolf Pack.
edges.evidence_quote: The Dhak-Duhre coalition is known to have developed strong ties with the UN and waged a war against the Wolf Pack

descriptions: The Dhak-Duhre gang was in conflict with the Wolfpack Alliance over the murder of Gurmit Dhak.
edges.evidence_quote: Sukhveer 'Sukh' Dhak, the younger brother of Gurmit, believed that the Wolfpack gang and that Amero, Riach, and Jonathan Bacon in particular were the ones responsible for his brother's murder.

descriptions: Independent Soldiers were founding members of the Wolfpack Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2010, Naicker together with another Independent Soldier, James Riarch, became a founding member of the Wolfpack Alliance alongside Larry Amero of the Hells Angels and Jonathan Bacon of the Red Scorpions.

descriptions: Sukhvir Singh Deo was a member of both Independent Soldiers and Wolfpack.
edges.evidence_quote: An Independent Soldier and Wolfpack member, Sukhvir Singh Deo moved to Toronto...

descriptions: Wolfpack Alliance is listed as a rival of United Nations in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Wolfpack Alliance

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Wolfpack Alliance , Red Scorpions , Brothers Keepers

edges.evidence_quote: The United Nations gang came to be involved in a violent feud with the Wolfpack Alliance led by Larry Amero and Rabih "Robby" Alkhalil of the Alkhalil family.

descriptions: Shane Maloney, a West End Gang leader, was a key member of the Wolfpack Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Maloney became a key member in the Wolfpack Alliance.

descriptions: The Alkhalil family joined the Wolfpack, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Wolfpack soon took in other members, most notably the Alkhalil family

descriptions: Alkhalil family is listed as an ally of the Wolfpack Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Alkhalil family

descriptions: Rabih Alkhalil, a member of the Alkhalil family, was a leader of the Wolfpack Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Rabih 'Robby' Alkhalil relocated to Montreal... Alkhalil was the leader of the Wolfpack Alliance alongside Larry Amero.

descriptions: Jonathan Bacon was a founding member of the Wolfpack Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Jonathan Bacon together with Larry Amero of the Hells Angels and James Riach and Randy Naicker of the Independent Soldiers formed a group called the Wolfpack Alliance in 2010.

descriptions: Wolfpack was believed responsible for a murder, leading to conflict with Dhak-Duhre group.
edges.evidence_quote: Suhhveer 'Sukh' Dhak, the younger brother of Gurmit, believed that the Wolfpack Alliance were the ones responsible for his brother's murder, and vowed to avenge him.

edges.evidence_quote: The senior partner in the Wolfpack was Amero. The Hells Angels allowed their members to have 'side operations' provided that the rest of the chapter is informed and allowed a cut of the profits.

descriptions: Wolfpack was founded by a Hells Angels member as a side project, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Wolfpack Alliance was founded in 2010 in the Lower Mainland of British Columbia by the Hells Angels member Larry Amero as a 'side project'.

edges.evidence_quote: The other leaders of the Wolfpack Alliance were Jonathan Bacon of the Red Scorpions

edges.evidence_quote: The Wolfpack became involved in a gang war in Toronto in 2016-2018 with the Siderno Group

descriptions: Sinaloa Cartel bypassed Wolfpack, rendering them irrelevant.
edges.evidence_quote: By early 2014, the Sinaloa Cartel had cut out the middlemen in the form of the Wolfpack and started to distribute cocaine themselves in Canada

edges.evidence_quote: The Wolfpack were used by Guzmán and the Sinaloa Cartel to establish a drug distribution network in Canada

edges.evidence_quote: The United Nations gang aligned itself with the Dhak-Duhre group against the Wolfpack.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: United Nations

descriptions: The West End Gang member Shane Maloney was a key member of the Wolfpack Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: A key member of the Wolfpack was Shane Kenneth Maloney of the West End Gang which controls the Port of Montreal.

descriptions: Shane Maloney of the West End Gang was a member of the Wolfpack Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Other members of the Wolfpack Alliance were the Alkhalil family of Montreal and Ottawa; Johnny Raposo and Martino Caputo of Toronto; Shane Maloney of the Montreal's West End Gang; and Nick Nero, a drug smuggler from Niagara Falls.

descriptions: A West End Gang member was a key Wolfpack leader, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: A key member of the Wolfpack proved to be Shane Kenneth 'Wheels' Maloney of the West End Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: West End Gang

Camera di controllo.description: The Camera di Controllo is a collegial body of the 'Ndrangheta, a Mafia-type organization in Calabria, Italy. Established after the Second 'Ndrangheta war (1985-1991) to settle disputes and coordinate activities among clans. It is composed of leading members and functions as a hierarchical superordinate authority, similar to the Sicilian Mafia Commission.

descriptions: Camera di Controllo is a governing body within the 'Ndrangheta organization.
edges.evidence_quote: The Camera di Controllo (Board of Control), is a collegial body of the 'Ndrangheta

descriptions: Camera di Controllo is a commission of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: He became a member of Camera di Controllo, a provincial commission of the 'Ndrangheta formed at the end of the Second 'Ndrangheta war in September 1991.

descriptions: Camera di controllo is a governing body of 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: La Camera di controllo o Camera di compensazione sono degli organismi di gestione e controllo della 'Ndrangheta al di fuori della Calabria adottati sin dagli anni settanta.

descriptions: Camera di controllo in Canada was created to avoid conflicts with American Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: Fin dagli anni sessanta esiste in Canada una camera di controllo voluta dai boss italo-americani di origine calabrese Frank Costello e Albert Anastasia per evitare contrasti con Cosa Nostra americana.

descriptions: A Camera di Controllo in Canada coordinates activities and resolves disputes for the Siderno Group.
edges.evidence_quote: A Camera di Controllo also exists in Canada, according to 'confidential information' of the police 'which they believe to be reliable.' It consists of six or seven Toronto-area men, who co-ordinate activities and resolves disputes among the Siderno Group in Southern Ontario.

descriptions: Salvatore Greco served as first secretary of the Sicilian Mafia Commission.
edges.evidence_quote: Salvatore "Ciaschiteddu" Greco was the first ‘secretary’ of the Sicilian Mafia Commission

descriptions: Angelo La Barbera defied the Commission's authority, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Angelo La Barbera refused to recognize the authority of the Commission over his family altogether.

descriptions: Salvatore La Barbera, leader of the Palermo Centro family, was a member of the Commission.
edges.evidence_quote: Salvatore La Barbera became a member of the first Sicilian Mafia Commission when it was set up somewhere in 1958.

Mkhedrioni.description: Paramilitary group in Georgia created in 1989 by Jaba Ioseliani. Involved in the Georgian Civil War and War in Abkhazia, known for criminal activities such as banditry, extortion, and smuggling. Outlawed in 1995 after being reconstituted as a political party. The group's influence vanished after a crackdown following an assassination attempt on President Shevardnadze.

descriptions: Mkhedrioni opposed the Bagramyan Battalion.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Gamsakhurdia's government in exile , Abkhazia , Bagramyan Battalion , Confederation of Mountain Peoples of the Caucasus

edges.target: Confederation of Mountain Peoples of the Caucasus
descriptions: Mkhedrioni opposed the Confederation of Mountain Peoples of the Caucasus.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Gamsakhurdia's government in exile , Abkhazia , Bagramyan Battalion , Confederation of Mountain Peoples of the Caucasus

descriptions: Mkhedrioni was led by a former thief-in-law, indicating ties to the Georgian mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: the Mkhedrioni led by former thief-in-law Jaba Ioseliani

Red Brigades.description: Italian far-left Marxist-Leninist militant group formed in 1970, responsible for numerous violent attacks during Italy's Years of Lead, including the kidnapping and murder of former prime minister Aldo Moro in 1978. The group sought to create a revolutionary state through armed struggle and remove Italy from NATO. It was broken up in the 1980s but had a resurgence in the late 1990s to 2000s.

descriptions: Banda della Magliana controlled the area where the Brigate Rosse held Aldo Moro prisoner.
edges.evidence_quote: il 16 marzo del 1978, quando il Presidente della DC Aldo Moro venne rapito da un commando di brigatisti, gran parte della città di Roma era controllata dagli uomini della Banda della Magliana e la stessa ubicazione della prigione del popolo ... era posto tra via Portuense e via della Magliana

descriptions: The Czechoslovak StB allegedly provided weapons, training, and logistic support to the Red Brigades.
edges.evidence_quote: Romanian defector Ion Mihai Pacepa claimed that the Red Brigades' primary foreign support came from the Czechoslovak StB and the Palestine Liberation Organization (PLO).

descriptions: The text describes ETA as a like-minded organization to the Red Brigades.
edges.evidence_quote: and, in Spain, the Basque left-wing nationalist group ETA

descriptions: The text describes the Irish National Liberation Army as a like-minded organization to the Red Brigades.
edges.evidence_quote: Like-minded organizations were the Red Army Faction in Germany, the Irish National Liberation Army (with their political wing, the Irish Republican Socialist Party) in Ireland

descriptions: The Italian Communist Party isolated the Red Brigades.
edges.evidence_quote: and were isolated from the Italian political left, including by the Italian Communist Party (PCI)

descriptions: Lotta Continua opposed the Red Brigades.
edges.evidence_quote: Throughout their existence, the BR were generally opposed by other far-left groups, such as Lotta Continua and Potere Operaio

descriptions: The Red Brigades killed two members of the MSI in a raid.
edges.evidence_quote: In June 1974, the Red Brigades killed two members of the Italian neo-fascist party, Movimento Sociale Italiano (MSI), during a raid on the MSI headquarters in Padua.

descriptions: The PLO allegedly provided training and support to the Red Brigades.
edges.evidence_quote: Romanian defector Ion Mihai Pacepa claimed that the Red Brigades' primary foreign support came from the Czechoslovak StB and the Palestine Liberation Organization (PLO).

descriptions: Potere Operaio opposed the Red Brigades.
edges.evidence_quote: Throughout their existence, the BR were generally opposed by other far-left groups, such as Lotta Continua and Potere Operaio

descriptions: The text describes the Red Army Faction as a like-minded organization to the Red Brigades.
edges.evidence_quote: Like-minded organizations were the Red Army Faction in Germany

descriptions: UDBA allegedly supported the Red Brigades as part of its operations to destabilize NATO.
edges.evidence_quote: According to Pacepa, support for the Red Brigades was a major part of the operations of UDBA, the intelligence service of non-aligned communist Yugoslavia.

Sumiyoshi-kai.description: The Sumiyoshi-kai is the second-largest yakuza group in Japan, based in Tokyo, with an estimated 2,100 members. It operates primarily in upscale districts such as Kabukichō and Ginza, engaging in protection rackets. Founded in 1958 by Jusaku Abe, it is a confederation of smaller gangs with a looser chain of command than its rival, the Yamaguchi-gumi. The group has undergone several name changes and reorganizations, and remains active as of 2020.

descriptions: Sumiyoshi-kai is part of the Kanto-Hatsukakai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: a federation of Tokyo-based yakuza groups including the Inagawa-kai and the Sumiyoshi-kai

descriptions: Sumiyoshi-kai was a member group of the Kanto-kai coalition.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization was formed of seven yakuza groups (including the Sumiyoshi-kai and Matsuba-kai)

descriptions: Kokusui-kai and Sumiyoshi-kai were engaged in shootings and retaliations over territory.
edges.evidence_quote: The suicide followed a rash of shootings and retaliations between Kokusui-kai and a rival gang, Sumiyoshi-kai, over gang territory, including the shooting death of Sumiyoshi-kai's boss.

descriptions: Matsuba-kai and Sumiyoshi-kai are both members of the Kantō Hatsuka-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Matsuba-kai is a member of a yakuza fraternal federation named the Kantō Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kantō -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Toa-kai , and the Soai-kai .

descriptions: Soai-kai and Sumiyoshi-kai are both dominating yakuza groups in Chiba Prefecture, but no specific relationship is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: Headquartered in Ichihara, Chiba , the Soai-kai is one of the three dominating yakuza groups in Chiba Prefecture , along with the Sumiyoshi-kai and the Inagawa-kai .

descriptions: Sumiyoshi-kai and Inagawa-kai are part of a loose alliance among major yakuza syndicates.
edges.evidence_quote: In recent years, the three major yakuza syndicates have formed a loose alliance, and in April 2023, Kiyoshi Takayama, the wakagashira (second-in-command) of the Yamaguchi-gumi, Shuji Ogawa, the kaichō (chairman) of the Sumiyoshi-kai, and Kazuya Uchibori, the kaichō of the Inagawa-kai, held a social gathering.

descriptions: Nishida-gumi is an affiliated group of Sumiyoshi-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: Takao Nishida, head of the affiliated Nishida-gumi, was gunned down

descriptions: Sumiyoshi-kai was founded by a leader of Sumiyoshi-ikka.
edges.evidence_quote: It was founded in 1958 as the Minato-kai (港会) by Jusaku Abe who was the 3rd sōchō ( 総長 ; "Supreme Leader") of the Sumiyoshi-ikka.

descriptions: Sumiyoshi-kai is a principal clan of the Yakuza.
edges.evidence_quote: Notable members: Principal clans: Yamaguchi-gumi , Sumiyoshi-kai , Inagawa-kai

descriptions: The text states Sumiyoshi-kai differs structurally from its main rival, Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: The Sumiyoshi-kai, as a federation, has a looser chain of command and while there is a chairman, some power is delegated to affiliate clan leaders.

descriptions: Shootings occurred between Sumiyoshi-kai and Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Police stated that they were probing for possible links between the murder and a series of shootings between the Sumiyoshi-kai and the Yamaguchi-gumi in neighboring Tochigi Prefecture.

descriptions: Retaliatory shooting by Sumiyoshi-kai against Yamaguchi-gumi offices.
edges.evidence_quote: Within hours the offices of the Yamaguchi-gumi were fired upon in retribution.

descriptions: Tensions raised between the two groups after a death.
edges.evidence_quote: raising tensions between the Sumiyoshi-kai and the Yamaguchi-gumi

descriptions: Toa-kai and Sumiyoshi-kai are fellow members of the Kanto Hatsuka-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Toa-kai is a member of a bakuto fraternal federation named the Kanto Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kanto -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Matsuba-kai , and the Soai-kai .

descriptions: Yamaguchi-gumi and Sumiyoshi-kai are part of a loose alliance among major yakuza syndicates.
edges.evidence_quote: In recent years, the three major yakuza syndicates have formed a loose alliance, and in April 2023, Kiyoshi Takayama, the wakagashira (second-in-command) of the Yamaguchi-gumi, Shuji Ogawa, the kaichō (chairman) of the Sumiyoshi-kai, and Kazuya Uchibori, the kaichō of the Inagawa-kai, held a social gathering.

descriptions: Black Sheep MC is listed as a rival of Bullshit MC in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Hells Angels MC Morticians MC, Black Sheep MC, Galloping Goose MC (Denmark)

descriptions: Black Sheep were a Hells Angels prospect club that carried out a murder on behalf of Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: Anker Walther "Høvding" Marcus (January 17, 1947 – December 21, 1985) was then murdered by Ole Bonnesen Nielsen and Rene Nøddeskov Ludvigsen, two members of the Black Sheep (another Hells Angels prospect club), following a Christmas party at Nemoland Café in Christiania on December 21, 1985.

descriptions: ARDE Frente Sur is a constituent group of the Contras.
edges.evidence_quote: Groups: F.D.N., A.R.D.E. Frente Sur, YATAMA, Misura

descriptions: A.R.D.E. Frente Sur is a constituent group of the Contras.
edges.evidence_quote: Groups: F.D.N., A.R.D.E. Frente Sur, YATAMA, Misura

Texis Cartel.description: The Texis Cartel was a Salvadoran criminal organization specializing in drug trafficking, transporting drugs from Colombian cartels through northern El Salvador to North America. Founded in the 1990s by businessman José Adán Salazar Umaña, politician Juan Umaña Samayoa, and businessman Roberto Herrera, it bribed law enforcement and judicial officials. The cartel was effectively dismantled by Salvadoran authorities in 2018 after several leaders were arrested.

descriptions: Texis Cartel hired 18th Street gang members for intimidation and killings.
edges.evidence_quote: The cartel also hired gang members from MS-13 and 18th Street gang to intimidate and kill opponents if necessary.

descriptions: Texis Cartel transported drugs manufactured by Colombian drug cartels.
edges.evidence_quote: The cartel transported drugs manufactured by Colombian drug cartels through northern El Salvador towards North America.

descriptions: Texis Cartel worked with Colombian drug cartels.
edges.evidence_quote: It typically worked with drug cartels from Colombia and Mexico.

descriptions: Infobox lists Colombian drug cartels as allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Colombian drug cartels , Mexican drug cartels

descriptions: Both groups received protection from Salvadoran officials, but no direct relationship between them is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: the United Nations confirmed that the Texis Cartel, along with the Los Perrones drug trafficking group, had received protection from Salvadoran government officials.

descriptions: Texis Cartel hired MS-13 members for intimidation and killings.
edges.evidence_quote: The cartel also hired gang members from MS-13 and 18th Street gang to intimidate and kill opponents if necessary.

descriptions: Texis Cartel worked with Mexican drug cartels.
edges.evidence_quote: It typically worked with drug cartels from Colombia and Mexico.

descriptions: Infobox lists Mexican drug cartels as allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Colombian drug cartels , Mexican drug cartels

descriptions: The UVF carried out campaigns against the IRA.
edges.evidence_quote: Loyalist paramilitaries, including the UVF, carried out campaigns aimed at thwarting the IRA's aspirations and maintaining the political union with Britain.

descriptions: The UVF is listed as an opponent of the Provisional IRA in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: United Kingdom British Army, Royal Ulster Constabulary, Ulster loyalist paramilitaries Ulster Volunteer Force, Ulster Defence Association

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: United Kingdom British Army, Royal Ulster Constabulary, Ulster loyalist paramilitaries Ulster Volunteer Force, Ulster Defence Association

descriptions: Tiandihui is descended from the White Lotus Sect.
edges.evidence_quote: the Tiandihui's ancestral organisation

descriptions: Triads were influenced by White Lotus societies.
edges.evidence_quote: some influenced by white lotus societies of the 14th century

descriptions: Krieger Verwandt is listed as an ally of SAC in the infobox.

'Ndrina Giampà.description: The 'Ndrina Giampà is a powerful 'Ndrangheta clan based in the Nicastro district of Lamezia Terme, Italy. It emerged in the late 1980s under boss Francesco Giampà, known as 'Il professore', who won a bloody feud against a rival faction. The clan is involved in drug and arms trafficking and was a key player in the Lamezia Terme feud from 2002 to 2011. Francesco Giampà is currently serving a life sentence.

descriptions: Accorinti has ties with Giampà.
edges.evidence_quote: Gli accorinti hanno legami con la 'ndrina dei Giampà di Lamezia Terme

descriptions: The Giampà refused to help the Cracolici out of respect for the Bonavota.
edges.evidence_quote: i quali avrebbero chiesto aiuto ai Giampà , che non si sono resi disponibili in quanto non avrebbero voluto fare 'sgarbo' ai Bonavota

descriptions: Cerra-Torcasio-Gualtieri and Iannazzo-Giampà were opposing sides in the Faida di Lamezia Terme.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Lamezia Terme : prese il via nel 2000 e si protrae a fasi alterne fino al 2011, cosche protagoniste sono i Cerra-Torcasio-Gualtieri e gli Iannazzo - Giampà

descriptions: Giampà and Cannizzaro-Daponte are mentioned together as part of the same criminal network.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 12 novembre 2018 si conclude l'operazione Quinta Bolgia ... per la cosca degli Iannazzo-Daponte-Cannizzaro e Giampà che fanno riferimento a Vincenzo Torcasio

descriptions: Giampà and Cerra-Torcasio-Gualtieri were once a single organization but split and became rivals in a violent war.
edges.evidence_quote: A partire dal 2002, a seguito della sentenza del processo Primi passi i locali di 'ndrangheta dei Cerra-Torcasio-Gualtieri e quello dei Giampà, che un tempo costituivano un'unica organizzazione criminale, a seguito di una grave scissione interna diventano protagonisti di una cruenta guerra di mafia.

descriptions: Giampà and Torcasio-Gualtieri made peace in 2015 after a feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2015 con l'operazione Andromeda viene fatta luce sulla pace tra gli Iannazzo-Giampà ed i Torcasio-Gualtieri avvenuta anche grazie alla mediazione di clan del reggino.

descriptions: Dattilo is listed as an ally of Giampà in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: Dattilo, Iannazzo

descriptions: Iannazzo is listed as an ally of Giampà in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: Dattilo, Iannazzo

descriptions: Iannazzo is allied with Giampà.
edges.evidence_quote: alleata dei Giampà e Cannizzaro-Daponte

descriptions: Iannazzo and Giampà are allied in the feud.
edges.evidence_quote: tra i Torcasio-Gualtieri-Cerra e gli Iannazzo - Giampà

descriptions: Torcasio is in conflict with Giampà in the Lamezia Terme feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2000 scoppia una faida ( faida di Lamezia Terme ) che si trascina ancora oggi tra i Torcasio-Gualtieri-Cerra e gli Iannazzo - Giampà

descriptions: Torcasio made peace with Giampà in 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: Viene fatta luce anche sulla pace tra gli Iannazzo - Giampà ed i Torcasio-Gualtieri grazie alla mediazione di clan del reggino

descriptions: Gypsy Joker MC amalgamated with Mandamas MC.
edges.evidence_quote: Following a friendly amalgamation with the notorious South Australian formed Mandamas MC and Gypsy Joker MC.

Clan Lubrano-Ligato.description: Camorra clan operating in Pignataro Maggiore, Caserta province, founded in 1965 by Vincenzo Lubrano. Historically allied with the Nuvoletta clan, Cosa Nostra, and the Banda della Magliana. The Ligato faction remains active after the death of boss Raffaele Ligato in 2022.

descriptions: The Lubrano-Ligato clan is listed as a rival of the Casalesi clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Lubrano-Ligato clan

descriptions: Lubrano-Ligato committed murders on orders of Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Il pentito Abate, collaboratore di giustizia, ed il capoclan Ligato, a cavallo tra gli anni '70 e 90, commisero 477 omicidi, per ordine della Banda della Magliana

descriptions: Clan dei Casalesi killed Raffaele Lubrano, a member of Lubrano-Ligato.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2002, il clan dei Casalesi ha ucciso Raffaele Lubrano, figlio di Vincenzo

descriptions: Lubrano family had very close ties with the Corleonesi.
edges.evidence_quote: I rapporti tra la famiglia Lubrano e i Corleonesi erano così stretti che Riina partecipò da latitante al matrimonio di Gaetano Lubrano

descriptions: Lubrano-Ligato committed murders on orders of Clan Nuvoletta.
edges.evidence_quote: Il pentito Abate, collaboratore di giustizia, ed il capoclan Ligato, a cavallo tra gli anni '70 e 90, commisero 477 omicidi, per ordine della Banda della Magliana , di Cosa nostra e del clan Nuvoletta

descriptions: Lubrano family was very close to the Orlando clan.
edges.evidence_quote: la famiglia Lubrano è stata anche molto vicina agli Orlando

descriptions: Lubrano-Ligato committed murders on orders of Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: Il pentito Abate, collaboratore di giustizia, ed il capoclan Ligato, a cavallo tra gli anni '70 e 90, commisero 477 omicidi, per ordine della Banda della Magliana , di Cosa nostra e del clan Nuvoletta

descriptions: Corleonesi allied with Lubrano-Ligato clan.
edges.evidence_quote: si allearono con i clan camorristici dei Nuvoletta , Lubrano-Ligato e Gionta

descriptions: The Nuvoletta clan was allied with the Lubrano-Ligato clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Lubrano-Ligato clan (defunct)

descriptions: The Lubrano-Ligato clan is listed as an ally of the Polverino clan in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Nuvoletta clan , Orlando clan , Mallardo clan , Lubrano-Ligato clan , Licciardi clan , Cosa Nostra

descriptions: Cosa Nostra's Corleonesi faction had contacts with the Lubrano-Ligato Camorra clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Elementi di spicco dei Corleonesi (come Salvatore Riina, Leoluca Bagarella, Bernardo Provenzano, Pippo Calò, Giovanni Brusca e Gaspare Mutolo) si sono trovati a contatto con famiglie camorristiche come i Nuvoletta, Lubrano-Ligato e Gionta, invece i rivali Carmine Alfieri e Antonio Bardellino, erano alleati dei perdenti come Alfio Ferlito, Gaetano Badalamenti e Tommaso Buscetta.

descriptions: TTP sent fighters to Syria to fight alongside Ansar al-Tawhid.
edges.evidence_quote: In July of 2013, the TTP's media wing, the Umar Media, had claimed to have set up training camps and to have sent hundreds of fighters to Syria to fight alongside the Syrian rebel groups and Islamist - Jihadist groups opposed to Bashar al-Assad such as the Al-Nusra Front, Ahrar al-Sham and Ansar al-Tawhid in an effort to strengthen ties with Al Qaeda against the government of Pakistan in the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Insurgency.

Di Lauro clan.description: The Clan Di Lauro is a Camorra organization based in the northern neighborhoods of Naples, Italy, specifically Secondigliano and Scampia. Founded by Paolo Di Lauro in 1982, it became one of the most powerful drug trafficking groups in Europe, at its peak earning over €500,000 per day from drug sales. The clan controlled the open-air drug market in Scampia's Vele district and was involved in a violent internal feud starting in 2004. Despite arrests of key members, the clan's influence persisted through alliances and family succession.

descriptions: Albanian organized crime groups supplied cocaine and hashish to a subgroup linked to the Di Lauro clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Italian authorities carried out a major anti-drug operation targeting links between Albanian organized crime groups and the Di Lauro clan , leading to the arrest of 14 people.

descriptions: The Di Lauro clan is a subgroup of the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: The Camorra's organizational structure is divided into individual groups called 'clans'.

descriptions: Di Lauro clan is a Camorra clan.
edges.evidence_quote: the Di Lauro clan has several links to the Sacra Corona Unita

descriptions: Di Lauro clan is a Camorra clan, part of the Camorra organization.
edges.evidence_quote: According to reports, in 2019, after the arrest of Marco Di Lauro , leader of the Di Lauro clan and fourth son of Paolo Di Lauro , the Contini clan became the most powerful clan of the Camorra

descriptions: Di Lauro clan is a constituent clan of the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: the Di Lauro clan, from Secondigliano, named after Paolo Di Lauro;

descriptions: The Abbinante clan was in a feud with the rival Di Lauro clan, involving many murders.
edges.evidence_quote: è il mandante di uno dei cinquanta e più omicidi che hanno insanguinato la città durante la faida con il clan rivale Di Lauro

descriptions: The Abbinante brothers were top members of the Di Lauro clan.
edges.evidence_quote: erano ritenuti dagli investigatori elementi di vertice del clan di spacciatori capeggiato da Paolo Di Lauro

descriptions: D'Alessandro was allied with Di Lauro.
edges.evidence_quote: dall'amico e alleato Paolo Di Lauro

descriptions: Pariante and others split from the Di Lauro clan, starting a conflict.

descriptions: Rosario Pariante helped Paolo Di Lauro found the Di Lauro clan, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Infatti, Pariante aiuterà Di Lauro a fondare il suo impero criminale, il clan Di Lauro.

descriptions: Di Lauro clan has an alliance with Contini clan regarding drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: The Di Lauro clan allegedly has an alliance with the Contini clan

descriptions: A Di Lauro subgroup maintained stable connections with Albanian criminal groups for drug supply.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization maintained stable connections with Albanian criminal groups, which supplied cocaine and hashish imported from abroad.

descriptions: Di Lauro clan is a constituent part of the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: The Di Lauro clan is an Italian crime clan, part of the Camorra in Naples.

descriptions: Abbinante clan boss was aligned with Di Lauro.
edges.evidence_quote: The Italian Direzione Distrettuale Antimafia first investigated the clan in 2002, which led to the imprisonment of its most influential members, including Abbinante clan boss Raffaele 'Papale e Marano' Abbinante, who was aligned with Di Lauro.

descriptions: The Di Lauro clan had a drug pact with the Contini clan.
edges.evidence_quote: il clan Di Lauro aveva un patto per questioni di droga con il clan Contini

descriptions: Giuliano clan is listed as a defunct ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Secondigliano Alliance Contini clan, Licciardi clan, Mallardo clan, Sacra Corona Unita, D'Alessandro clan, Giuliano clan (defunct), Albanian mafia

edges.evidence_quote: the Di Lauro clan has several links to the Sacra Corona Unita in question with regards to drug trafficking.

edges.evidence_quote: The Di Lauro clan also has ties to the Sacra Corona Unita and Ciro Contini, a member of the Contini Clan, regarding drug trafficking.

descriptions: The Di Lauro clan and the Scissionisti di Secondigliano fought a bloody feud.
edges.evidence_quote: the Di Lauro clan and the so-called Scissionisti di Secondigliano fought a bloody feud which came to be known ... as the Scampia feud.

descriptions: Scissionisti di Secondigliano are former allies turned rivals, engaged in a power struggle.
edges.evidence_quote: Some of Di Lauro's former allies formed a rival alliance (Scissionisti di Secondigliano) and started a bloody power struggle in 2004 known as the Scampia feud.

descriptions: Di Lauro clan and Scissionisti di Secondigliano were in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The Di Lauro clan war against the Scissionisti di Secondigliano inspired the current Italian television series Gomorrah.

descriptions: Di Lauro fought scissionist groups (Scissionisti di Secondigliano) in the first faida of Scampia from 2004 to 2005.
edges.evidence_quote: Prima faida di Scampia : guerra svoltasi tra l'ottobre 2004 e il settembre 2005 ... il conflitto si scatenò quando vari gruppi scissionisti del clan Di Lauro decisero di staccarsi dalla casa madre

edges.evidence_quote: prima faida di Scampia che vede contrapposti il clan Di Lauro contro quello degli Scissionisti

descriptions: Loyalists of Paolo Di Lauro left the clan and fought a war against it as the Scissionisti di Secondigliano.

descriptions: The Di Lauro family acts as external guarantors for the Alleanza di Secondigliano.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2023 Edin Gačanin was sanctioned by the US who cited "close ties" to the Irish Kinahan Organised Crime Group and a €350m cocaine plot.

descriptions: U.S. supporters of the Muslim Brotherhood started the American Muslim Council.
edges.evidence_quote: U.S. supporters of the Brotherhood also started other organizations including: ... the American Muslim Council in 1990

Grupos de Autodefensa Comunitaria.description: Civilian armed groups and resistance movement formed in 2013 in Mexico to combat drug cartels, primarily in Michoacán, Guerrero, and Jalisco. They emerged in response to violence and extortion by cartels such as La Familia Michoacana, Los Caballeros Templarios, and CJNG. Leaders include Hipólito Mora and José Manuel Mireles. The groups are decentralized and have been linked to rival cartels.

descriptions: Some Autodefensa leaders are directly linked to CJNG, and CJNG provided vehicles and weapons.
edges.evidence_quote: los líderes son vinculados directamente con el Cartel de Jalisco Nueva Generación como Juan José Farías Álvarez

descriptions: The Autodefensas formed to resist attacks by Los Caballeros Templarios.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Grupos de Autodefensa o Guardias Comunitarias son un grupo de civiles armados y un movimiento de resistencia contra grupos delictivos, mayoritariamente aguacateros o limoneros que toman las armas para defenderse de los ataques de los cárteles mexicanos, más notablemente La Familia Michoacana , Los Caballeros Templarios

descriptions: The Autodefensas formed to resist attacks by La Familia Michoacana.
